title: Audio Track Study Shinji SDAT Song and background voices from Shinji breaks down
display_title: Audio Track Study Shinji SDAT Song and background voices from Shinji breaks down
wikitext:
[]: [Audio Track Study] Shinji SDAT Song & background voices from Shinji breaks down             Quote Post by Wanderer » Sun May 30, 2021 11:35 am Hi all, new fish here. Although the audio tracks are quite chaotic, it is still an excellent primary source. For example, Shinji breaks down in 3.0 (Q). It has been found that reused some of the EOE audios. (not 100% sure, but the tone and speech tempo are difficult to reproduce.) Anyway, let's back to the topic and make it quick. Shinji SDAT Song Source: 1.11(BD 6.1[7]ch), 2.22(BD 6.1[7]ch), 3.33(BD 5.1[6]ch), all FLAC Summary: always playing one and only one song - "Blue Legend" (蒼いレジェンド Aoi Legend), sad no other songs Proofs: 1.11, 32:27-32:40, Shinji first time sleep in Misato's apartment SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: [Audio Track Study] Shinji SDAT Song & background voices from Shinji breaks down             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun May 30, 2021 3:57 pm The audio bits from EoE were spotted already - Basically they are various lines of rejection, "girlfriend dumps you" type of dialogue. In the context of EoE it probably represents people not really liking/ welcoming instrumentality (represented as rejecting a more every day sort of union) - & Asuka's voice is the last we hear cause she resisted most of all. a) She hates Shinji most of anyone and doesn't want to be merged with him b) She really does not want to "disappear"/"lose her individuality" / die - after all she fought for survival to the last But there are other ppl among the 3 billion that must have really rejected the ritual suicide of sorts (Aoba for example couldn't be gotten to let down his guard and had to be forced into the union kicking and screaming) so I figure that personally knowing the 'lynchpin' of the process was a factor. But it probably also signifies Shinji looking at the old world as a place of rejection that he gleefully takes petty vengeance on through instrumentality, at least at this point before he ends up reconsidering. In the context of Q, the scene is already a barrage of actual or perceived rejection/harsh treatment. Shinji feels like everyone hates him/abandoned him & he can't trust anybody any longer, & that everyone had rejected him. The sequence already has a barrage of scenes that he perceives as lines/expressions of rejection, mixing in a "background radiation" of generic rejection is probably supposed to add onto that feeling - Shinji doesn't just feel like these particular people reject him, but that everyone rejects him. I heard the scene got a slight makeover in 3.333 and I can't wait to see it Though using the same "generic rejection lines" as EoE is most certainly a sort of meta point. Thanks for going through all this work though! I would like you to elaborate on what you mentioned about Yui audio, I didn't spot any, what does she say?

[]: Re: [Audio Track Study] Shinji SDAT Song & background voices from Shinji breaks down             Quote Post by Wanderer » Mon May 31, 2021 11:06 am Thanks Kendrix. Kendrix wrote: In the context of Q, the scene is already a barrage of actual or perceived rejection/harsh treatment. Shinji feels like everyone hates him/abandoned him & he can't trust anybody any longer, & that everyone had rejected him. The sequence already has a barrage of scenes that he perceives as lines/expressions of rejection, mixing in a "background radiation" of generic rejection is probably supposed to add onto that feeling - Shinji doesn't just feel like these particular people reject him, but that everyone rejects him.

Article: Audio Track Study Shinji SDAT Song and background voices from Shinji breaks down
 I figure that personally knowing the 'lynchpin' of the process was a factor. But it probably also signifies Shinji looking at the old world as a place of rejection that he gleefully takes petty vengeance on through instrumentality, at least at this point before he ends up reconsidering. In the context of Q, the scene is already a barrage of actual or perceived rejection/harsh treatment. Shinji feels like everyone hates him/abandoned him & he can't trust anybody any longer, & that everyone had rejected him. The sequence already has a barrage of scenes that he perceives as lines/expressions of rejection, mixing in a "background radiation" of generic rejection is probably supposed to add onto that feeling - Shinji doesn't just feel like these particular people reject him, but that everyone rejects him. I heard the scene got a slight makeover in 3.333 and I can't wait to see it Though using the same "generic rejection lines" as EoE is most certainly a sort of meta point. Thanks for going through all this work though! I would like you to elaborate on what you mentioned about Yui audio, I didn't spot any, what does she say?
